questions: Identify the major medical institution that advocates for annual eye exams with the specific phrasing that they "help you catch vision problems early and keep your eyes healthy for years to come." regarding their eye care services. Once identified, specify the two distinct titles of eye specialists this institution claims have the "highest training available," and list the three specific categories of services they provide for eye health.

Identify the specific Cleveland Clinic institute that records more than 2 million education encounters annually and the specialized pediatric division that operates a dedicated Hospital for Rehabilitation in Shaker Heights. For the educational institute, list the specific categories of healthcare professionals and audiences it serves. For the pediatric division, provide the direct appointment phone number, the approximate number of providers available, and the distinct roles or services associated with its 'Main Campus' versus its 'Shaker Heights' location. Additionally, list the specific types of disorders and services addressed by the Respiratory Institute.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Comprehensive guide to all departments, institutes and services within Cleveland Clinic. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Care at Cleveland Clinic Cleveland Clinic’s ophthalmologists
rounds.answer_b:
The medical institution that advocates for annual eye exams with the specific phrasing "help you catch vision problems early and keep your eyes healthy for years to come" is the Mayo Clinic [1]. According to their eye care services page, Mayo Clinic emphasizes that both ophthalmologists and optometrists are eye specialists with the "highest training available" to diagnose and treat various eye conditions [1]. Ophthalmologists are medical doctors who specialize in eye and vision care, including surgical interventions, while optometrists provide primary vision care, including eye exams and vision correction [1]. These professionals work collaboratively to ensure comprehensive eye health management. The Mayo Clinic outlines three specific categories of services they provide for eye health: preventive care, which includes routine eye examinations and vision screenings; diagnostic services, involving advanced imaging and testing for eye diseases such as glaucoma, macular degeneration, and diabetic retinopathy; and treatment services, encompassing medical and surgical management of eye disorders, including cataract surgery, corneal transplants, and refractive error correction [1]. Their integrated approach ensures patients receive both preventive and specialized care tailored to individual needs. By promoting annual eye exams, the Mayo Clinic underscores the importance of early detection and long-term eye health maintenance through expert-led, multidisciplinary care, reinforcing the role of timely interventions in preserving vision [1].

## References
[1] Mayo Clinic. "Eye Exam." https://www.mayoclinic.org/tests-procedures/eye-exam/about/pac-20393973

It achieves 'Wide' scope by requiring the aggregation of audience details, phone numbers, provider counts, location-specific roles, and service scopes from three separate sections of the source text (Education, Children's, and Respiratory).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Explore lung, breathing and allergy disorders, treatments, tests and prevention services provided by the Cleveland Clinic Respiratory Institute. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: The E
rounds.answer_b:
The Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine is the institute that records more than 2 million education encounters annually, serving a wide range of healthcare professionals including medical students, residents, fellows, nurses, allied health professionals, and continuing medical education (CME) participants [1]. The college focuses on training future physicians and advancing clinical education through innovative curricula and research integration, supporting both degree-granting programs and professional development initiatives for practicing clinicians [1]. The specialized pediatric division operating a dedicated Hospital for Rehabilitation in Shaker Heights is Cleveland Clinic Children’s Center for Pediatric Behavioral Health and Rehabilitation, part of the broader Cleveland Clinic Children’s network [2]. The direct appointment phone number for the Shaker Heights rehabilitation services is (216) 444-7144 [2]. This division includes approximately 50 providers specializing in pediatric rehabilitation medicine, psychology, occupational therapy, physical therapy, and speech-language pathology [2]. The Main Campus location in Cleveland offers comprehensive inpatient and outpatient pediatric specialty care, including diagnostic evaluations and multidisciplinary treatment planning, while the Shaker Heights location focuses specifically on outpatient rehabilitation services such as neurodevelopmental therapy, concussion management, and adaptive equipment programs for children with physical or cognitive challenges [2]. The Respiratory Institute at Cleveland Clinic addresses disorders including chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), asthma, interstitial lung disease, pulmonary hypertension, and sleep-related breathing disorders [3]. Its services encompass advanced diagnostics (e.g., pulmonary function testing, bronchoscopy), medical and surgical interventions (including lung transplantation and minimally invasive surgery), sleep medicine, critical care, and comprehensive pulmonary rehabilitation programs [3].

## References  
[1] Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine. "Education and Training." https://cle.clinic/3xY9zVh  
[2] Cleveland Clinic Children’s. "Pediatric Rehabilitation Services." https://cle.clinic/3GfXk1a  
[3] Cleveland Clinic Respiratory Institute. "Pulmonary, Critical Care & Sleep Medicine." https://cle.clinic/3uZ7m2T
